title: Detection and spectrophotometric detennination of paracetamol with vanadium(V)
author: B. Sreerama Murthy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Maharajah's Post Graduate College, Vizianagaram-535 002, Andhra Pradesh, India 
 E-mail  : sreeram6000@gmail.com, prs_ganti@yahoo.co.in 
 Manuscript received 30 June 2010, revised 27 October 2010, accepted 03 November 2010  

 A simple and accurate method was developed for the detection and determination of paracetamol by spectrophotometry 
in pharmaceutical formulations in the form of tablets. Paracetamol, was dissolved in 4 M sulphuric acid and treated with 1% aqueous sodium vanadate solution in the presence of 1 M hydrochloric acid. It exhibited a stable bluish-violet colour. The coloured compound showed λ max  at 560 nm. This method is also recommended as a spot test for paracetamol. It is also precise and found to be accurate for qualitative and quantitative determinations of paracetamol.
